description:
Add crates/balls-plugin-github-issues/ with main.rs, cli.rs matching the
plugin protocol surface (auth-setup, auth-check, push, sync), and command
modules that noop. Wired to balls-github-shared for auth/http/config.
Reads from .balls/local/plugins/github-issues/ (separate auth-dir per
Epic B's auth decision).

WHY: a skeleton that registers and is invoked but does nothing is the
smallest possible proof that the participant-protocol wiring is right.
Every subsequent child adds *behavior*; this child proves the
*invocation*. Splitting the wire-up from the behavior is what makes the
behavior-children's diffs reviewable.

ACCEPTANCE:
- balls-plugin-github-issues binary builds and is installed by
  `make install` (Makefile updated).
- auth-setup reads a token from stdin and writes
  <auth-dir>/token.json, mode 0600 (via shared::auth, parameterized).
- auth-check returns 0 if GET /user succeeds with the stored token,
  non-zero otherwise.
- push --task ID prints `{}` (silent noop, per Plugin Protocol's
  "off-path tasks are no-ops" convention).
- sync (no --task) prints `{"created":[],"updated":[],"deleted":[]}`.
- 100% coverage; every file under 300 lines.
title: Binary skeleton: CLI, main, participant registration (noop on all commands)
